narrative:
Temperatures take a step up today, and dads will enjoy quite a bit of sunshine, mixed with some afternoon cloudiness, for this Father's Day + first day of summer combo. Despite the very warm temperatures and lots of sunshine, we will remain well shy of record highs, and humidity levels should be quite comfortable. This quiet stretch of weather gives way to our next shot at meaningful rain Monday afternoon.

Fine Father's Day

Last Updated Sun June 21 at 9:37AM by Alan Simmons

Temperatures take a step up today, and dads will enjoy quite a bit of sunshine, mixed with some afternoon cloudiness, for this Father's Day + first day of summer combo. Despite the very warm temperatures and lots of sunshine, we will remain well shy of record highs, and humidity levels should be quite comfortable. This quiet stretch of weather gives way to our next shot at meaningful rain Monday afternoon.

2027 Blue Ridge Calendar Pre-Publication Sale is Underway!

The 2027 Blue Ridge Calendar is being printed now and will be available around July 1. Until then, the Pre-Publication Sale is underway with the best price we will offer all year. Forecast Discussion

Nice weather sticks around for one more day, giving us a great finish to the holiday weekend. A hardly noticeable warm front will push back north through the area today and that will give us a boost in temperatures and some clouds to go along with the sunshine this afternoon.

Clouds and humidity increase tonight ahead of our next summertime cool front. The front brings us rainfall in the form of showers and thunderstorms, which should arrive Monday afternoon and last well into the evening. Rainfall amounts overall don't look tremendous with this next front, but it will help the ongoing drought situation, and we could see a couple of spots wind up with some decent totals. A few showers linger into Tuesday behind the front, mostly along the western escarpment, but these are nothing to write home about and will fade away during the day.

Weak high pressure builds back in for a nicer day Wednesday. This being late June, you know we can't hold dry, stable air off for long. Increasing moisture surges back into the area on Thursday as stubborn high pressure sets up shop over the western Atlantic. This leads to a daily scattering of mostly afternoon and evening showers or thundershowers each day Thursday through Saturday.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.

WWR? Wolf Laurel!

<video controls src="https://d3efbyumrgw1ny.cloudfront.net/Jun-18-2026-1781795436_0780511.MOV" width="400"></video>

Jun 18, 2026
Yadkin Valley Deluge

A morning complex of thunderstorms moving across the Ohio Valley and into West Virginia and Virginia produced an outflow boundary that pushed southward into North Carolina during the afternoon of June 18. This outflow boundary, the result of a cool rush of air from thunderstorms occurring to the north, served as an effective focusing mechanism for training thunderstorms that occurred over parts of Surry, northern Wilkes and southern Alleghany County during Thursday afternoon and evening.

This occurred under the backdrop of increasing tropical moisture returning from the southwest as the remnants of Tropical Storm Arthur moved into Alabama.

The result was excessive rainfall, in the range of 2-4 inches, with locally higher amounts of around 5 inches in central Surry County.

Jun 19, 2026
Monday Message for June 15, 2026

It's the pre-summer solstice edition of the Monday Message, available for subscribers. El Niño has officially arrived, and will have significant implications for our weather heading into fall and winter.
